The '''YZ-775 medium transport''' was a [[Transport/Legends|transport]] in the [[YZ series]] of [[Starship/Legends|ships]] produced by the [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]].

==Characteristics==
The cockpit was an oversized variant of the standard conical CEC cockpit, capable of seating all 8 crew members. Aft of the cockpit the ship had a two-deck layout, split between the cargo hold and crew quarters.

The YZ-775 was armed with a double [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] turret, two double [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]] turrets, and two [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] tubes. This layout was deemed excessive for a non-combatant transport by Imperial agencies, a claim CEC denied as it had properly gained authorization for such a layout from the Imperial Bureau of Shipyards and Construction.

At least one, the ''[[No Luck Required]]'', was modified to remove some of its weaponry and replace it with four [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wings]] on a rotating rack.<ref name="Emissary" />

==History==
[[File:YZ-775_-_Dangerous_Covenant.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A YZ-775 landing in battle]]
It was first produced shortly after the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The 52-meter-long vessel was one of the largest to be classified as a "[[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]]," and could carry 400 metric tons of cargo. Its design bore some resemblance to the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] and [[DP20 gunship/Legends|Corellian gunship]] series.

The series was popular with [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and was favored by high-ranking [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] agents. Many also found their way into the forces of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]], and later served in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].
